evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses correct graph.facebook.com/v19.0/{phone_number_id}/messages endpoint, proper template payload structure, X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC-SHA256 verification with hmac.compare_digest. Internally consistent with version claims; long-tail handling applies.
evidence.executability: Code is split across many small fragments scattered through prose with no single coherent main.py. Multiple undefined symbols when extracted (get_settings never defined, Response/Depends imports missing in some snippets, Settings referenced across blocks). Pydantic v1 BaseSettings import path is outdated (should be pydantic_settings). Explicitly relies on ngrok for webhook testing — sandbox-infeasibility pattern matched, caps at 2.
evidence.completeness: (a) API key acquisition described in prose for Meta dashboard; (b) install commands mentioned in prose but not in clean ```bash blocks; (c) env vars listed in prose (WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN etc.) but no .env template block; (d) backend code fragments present but scattered; (e) frontend code present; (f) test commands shown as pytest snippets but no single runnable test command. Multiple pieces light/missing.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Citations are bracketed numeric references [1][2][7] etc. with no resolvable URLs to developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp. No official canonical URLs anchored near code. References appear to be third-party/unidentified.

rubric_notes: Extremely long, prose-heavy with code fragments interleaved. Deterministic extractor would struggle: many fenced blocks but no cohesive file. Heavy duplication of explanatory content penalizes completeness. ngrok dependence caps executability.
